summary: Comprehensive pre-deployment QA of Ace Chasers (backend + frontend). 39/40 pytest cases pass (1 skipped due to test sequencing, not a defect). All critical security and visibility paths verified end-to-end. UI flows for signup, feed compose, post visibility, discovery, profile edit, public profile route, logout, and protected-route gating all behave correctly.

minor.issue: Firebase Identity Toolkit lowercases email at signup, so the stored email may differ in case from the local form input. Not a defect — just informational; client comparisons should be case-insensitive.

ui_bugs.component: Discovery/Likes (LikesPage after liking a non-seed user)
ui_bugs.issue: After clicking the Like button on the first Discovery card in an automated run, the Likes page showed empty state. Could be a Playwright click-timing artifact (card slides out as the next card slides in) or a UI race in the Discovery store. Worth a manual repro: like a non-seed user, then navigate to /likes — the like should appear with 'Waiting for them to like back…'. The backend swipe endpoint itself is verified working in pytest.

issues: All seeded/test-author posts show 'Player' as the author name (because backend doesn't auto-populate name on Firebase-only signups that skip PUT /users/me). Real-user signups via the UI set the name correctly, so this only affects API-created accounts. Could fall back to email-prefix or 'Anonymous' instead of the generic 'Player'.

Many cards share the same default avatar image and default bio 'New to Ace Chasers!' (test data). Not a defect in production once real users sign up, but the test data was created during this QA run and persists in the DB.

action_items: Manually verify the LikesPage shows newly liked non-seed users (UI race suspected, backend confirmed OK).
OPTIONAL: cleanup TEST_ users + posts that this QA run created in the live ace_chasers Mongo DB (search for emails matching /^test_[a-f0-9]{8}@example.com/ and post bodies starting with 'TEST_').
OPTIONAL: change author fallback name from 'Player' to email prefix or 'Anonymous'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: ProfileIn (PUT /api/users/me) does not validate string length or list size — a malicious client could push a 10MB bio. Recommend adding pydantic Field(max_length=...) on bio (and name/location/etc.) and a cap on interests length.

server.py: image upload helpers (_save_image_for_user, create_post_endpoint) only check content_type header (client-supplied, spoofable) — a request that lies about content-type can write arbitrary bytes to disk. Recommend sniffing the file magic bytes or running through PIL to validate.

server.py: uploaded files are written with the *client-supplied* extension (sanitized but still attacker-influenced). Combined with the StaticFiles mount at /api/uploads/, a .html / .js could be served. Recommend forcing the extension to match the validated MIME type (jpg/png/webp/gif).

firebase_auth.py: still has the insecure dev-mode fallback that decodes JWTs without signature verification. In production the env var IS set, so the safe path runs, but the dead branch is a footgun if FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON ever gets unset or its path is wrong (it silently switches to insecure mode). Recommend failing closed in production via an explicit ENV=prod flag.
posts.py: get_friend_uids loops with await per match doc — fine for current scale, but should use a projection-only find for large match collections.
server.py is 593 lines (under 700) but is already mixing routes, models, helpers, and the image-save logic. Consider splitting into routers/ modules before further growth.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (one suspected UI race on Like→Likes transition)

test_credentials: Used Firebase Identity Toolkit REST API to mint real ID tokens for ephemeral TEST_ users (deleted in fixture teardown). For UI test, signed up as qa_<random>@example.com / demo1234 via the live Sign-Up form.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created ~30 test posts (bodies prefixed 'TEST_') and ~2 Firebase Auth users (auto-deleted on teardown). UI test created qa_454caffaed@example.com and qa_d12f9f0ab4@example.com via Firebase (NOT auto-deleted — recommend cleanup).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_api.py (40 tests, 39 pass, 1 conditional skip). Re-run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_api.py -v. The dev session fallback (devAuth.js) is currently DEAD CODE because Firebase Admin is active — any new tests must mint real Firebase ID tokens via Identity Toolkit (see _signup_firebase helper). Friends-only post leakage test passed (test_friends_only_invisible_to_stranger) — the visibility model in posts.list_feed correctly enforces the (own | public | friends_only AND author_uid IN friends) filter. Image upload validation passed (size + MIME). Admin endpoints are properly gated by X-Admin-Key. Email is correctly stripped from /api/users/{uid} for non-self callers.
rca of the issue: Suspected LikesPage UI race: after a successful POST /api/swipes, Discovery.jsx pops the card from the deck optimistically and (presumably) does not refetch /api/likes — so when the user navigates to /likes, the store might still hold a stale empty list if the page was visited before the first like. Worth checking Likes.jsx for whether it triggers a fetch on mount/route-enter, and whether matchStore is invalidated after a successful swipe.
